panels.description: Observability Export SLO chip for the selected range: one means no local telemetry drops were observed. If no workload was observed, this zero-safe query still renders healthy idle rather than no data.
panels.title: Export health

panels.description: Agent Lineage SLO chip for the selected range. A healthy value means subagent spawns are not accompanied by spawn failures, orphan agents, or trace-context propagation failures.

targets.expr: clamp_min(1 - (((sum(rate(observme_subagent_spawn_failures_total[$__range])) or vector(0)) + (sum(rate(observme_orphan_agents_total[$__range])) or vector(0)) + (sum(rate(observme_trace_context_propagation_failures_total[$__range])) or vector(0))) / clamp_min((sum(rate(observme_subagents_spawned_total[$__range])) or vector(0)), 1e-9)), 0)

panels.title: Agent lineage health

panels.description: Workflow Completion SLO chip for terminal telemetry. A healthy idle range with no started workflows renders as 100%; non-idle ranges compare completed plus explicitly failed workflows against started workflows.

targets.expr: clamp_max(((((sum(increase(observme_workflows_completed_total[$__range])) or vector(0)) + (sum(increase(observme_workflow_errors_total[$__range])) or vector(0))) / clamp_min((sum(increase(observme_workflows_started_total[$__range])) or vector(0)), 1)) + (((sum(increase(observme_workflows_started_total[$__range])) or vector(0)) == bool 0)), 1)

panels.title: Workflow completion health

panels.description: Current error pressure across workflow, agent, LLM, tool, and handler failures divided by the main workload counters. Zero can mean healthy idle or no failures in the selected interval.

targets.expr: (((sum(rate(observme_workflow_errors_total[$__rate_interval])) or vector(0)) + (sum(rate(observme_agent_run_errors_total[$__rate_interval])) or vector(0)) + (sum(rate(observme_llm_errors_total[$__rate_interval])) or vector(0)) + (sum(rate(observme_tool_failures_total[$__rate_interval])) or vector(0)) + (sum(rate(observme_handler_errors_total[$__rate_interval])) or vector(0))) / clamp_min(((sum(rate(observme_turns_completed_total[$__rate_interval])) or vector(0)) + (sum(rate(observme_llm_requests_total[$__rate_interval])) or vector(0)) + (sum(rate(observme_tool_calls_total[$__rate_interval])) or vector(0)) + (sum(rate(observme_agent_runs_total[$__rate_interval])) or vector(0))), 1e-9))

panels.title: Error pressure

panels.description: Estimated LLM cost burn per hour for the selected dashboard range. Yellow/red thresholds mirror the default cost-spike alert scale and should be tuned to deployment budget.
panels.title: Cost burn / hour

panels.description: p95 turn latency as the first user-facing latency health check. Zero can be healthy idle when no turn-duration buckets were observed.
panels.title: Latency health

panels.description: Estimated currently active sessions from started minus shutdown counters. This is an estimate and can reset with process restarts; zero can mean healthy idle.
panels.title: Active sessions estimate

panels.description: Completed turns per minute, shown as a compact KPI with sparkline instead of a full detail chart.
panels.title: Turns/min

panels.description: LLM requests per minute. Use Models or Cost for provider/model breakdowns.
panels.title: LLM requests/min

panels.description: Tool calls per minute. Open Tools for reliability and slow-tool breakdowns.
panels.title: Tool calls/min

panels.description: Subagent spawns per minute across all roles and depths. Open Agents or Trace Journey for fan-out and lineage details.
panels.title: Subagent spawns/min

panels.description: Currently active ObservMe agent runtimes. Zero can mean healthy idle; high values should be compared with fan-out/depth in the Agent lineage row.
panels.title: Active agents

panels.description: Total LLM cost over the selected dashboard range. Open Cost for provider/model attribution and budget details.
panels.title: Total cost

panels.description: Selected-range LLM token mix. Detailed provider/model token, cache, and reasoning analysis belongs in Cost, Models, and Logs and LLM I/O.
panels.title: Token mix

panels.description: Total LLM cost trend without provider/model detail. Open Cost or Models when this sparkline rises unexpectedly.
panels.title: Cost trend

panels.description: p95 turn latency trend with a compact sparkline. Open Trace Journey to find the slow stage for one request.
panels.title: p95 turn latency

panels.description: p95 LLM request latency trend. Open Models for provider/model comparison.
panels.title: p95 LLM latency

panels.description: p95 tool-call latency trend. Open Tools to find slow or failing tools.
panels.title: p95 tool latency

panels.description: Selected-range lineage failures: spawn failures, orphan agents, and trace-context propagation failures. Empty/zero is healthy for the range.

targets.expr: (sum(increase(observme_subagent_spawn_failures_total[$__range])) or vector(0)) + (sum(increase(observme_orphan_agents_total[$__range])) or vector(0)) + (sum(increase(observme_trace_context_propagation_failures_total[$__range])) or vector(0))

panels.title: Lineage failures

panels.description: p95 agent-tree depth. Yellow/red thresholds match the default excessive-depth alert guidance and should be tuned per deployment.
panels.title: p95 tree depth

panels.description: p95 fan-out per parent operation. The warning threshold starts at the documented runaway fan-out alert threshold of 20.
panels.title: p95 fan-out

panels.description: Current active agents by low-cardinality role and depth. Use this as a snapshot before opening the Agents dashboard for deeper breakdowns.
panels.title: Active agents by role/depth

panels.description: Operator navigation map. These links intentionally preserve the current Grafana time range.

options.content:
# Drill-down dashboards

Use this overview to decide where to go next, then open the focused dashboard with the same time range:

- [Cost](/d/observme-cost/observme-cost?${__url_time_range}) for spend, burn rate, token mix, and cache context.
- [Models](/d/observme-models/observme-models?${__url_time_range}) for provider/model request, error, latency, and stop-reason context.
- [Latency](/d/observme-latency/observme-latency?${__url_time_range}) for p95 trends and slow-stage investigation.
- [Tools](/d/observme-tools/observme-tools?${__url_time_range}) for tool volume, failures, and slow calls.
- [Agents](/d/observme-agents/observme-agents?${__url_time_range}) for subagent spawns, fan-out, depth, orphan, wait, and join behavior.
- [Trace Journey](/d/observme-trace-journey/observme-trace-journey?${__url_time_range}) for metric-to-log-to-trace workflow inspection.
- [Errors](/d/observme-errors/observme-errors?${__url_time_range}) for current error-family pressure and logs.
- [Export Health](/d/observme-export-health/observme-export-health?${__url_time_range}) for telemetry drops, redaction failures, export failures, and handler health.
- [LLM Conversations](/d/observme-llm-conversations/observme-llm-conversations?${__url_time_range}) for redacted opt-in prompt, response, and thinking timelines.

panels.title: Dashboard links
